question_text: The module calls the Holy Spirit "The New Roommate." According to the text, why is this name used?
answer_options: Because a roommate shares your space and is present in your everyday life
Because a roommate only visits occasionally when invited
Because a roommate is a force that comes and goes
Because a roommate appears only during church services
correct_answer: Because a roommate shares your space and is present in your everyday life

question_text: The text describes who the Holy Spirit is in relation to the Trinity. How does it identify Him?
answer_options: Fully God, the third person of the Trinity, co-equal with the Father and the Son
A power and a feeling given by the Father
An angel sent to appear to mankind
A lesser spirit subordinate to the Father and the Son
correct_answer: Fully God, the third person of the Trinity, co-equal with the Father and the Son

question_text: The module lists personal traits of the Holy Spirit to show He has a personality. Which set of traits does it give?
answer_options: He loves, He gets angry, and He gets heartbreaks and grief
He is happy, calm, and never disturbed
He is wise, powerful, and unfeeling
He loves, He forgives, and He rejoices only
correct_answer: He loves, He gets angry, and He gets heartbreaks and grief

question_text: The text explains when the Holy Spirit takes up residence in a person, making them a living temple. When does this happen?
answer_options: At the moment of the new birth
After years of faithful church attendance
Only when a believer first speaks in tongues
When a believer earns it through human effort
correct_answer: At the moment of the new birth

question_text: The module distinguishes between the gifts and the fruit of the Holy Spirit. According to the text, what is true of the gifts versus the fruit?
answer_options: Gifts are given as He wills to anyone He chooses, while fruits abide even when gifts are not visibly at work
Fruits are given as He wills, while gifts abide permanently in every believer
Both gifts and fruits are natural talents God may use
Gifts are produced by hard work, while fruits are received by faith
correct_answer: Gifts are given as He wills to anyone He chooses, while fruits abide even when gifts are not visibly at work

question_text: The text says the ministry of the Holy Spirit is not to draw attention to Himself. What does it say He does instead?
answer_options: He glorifies Jesus and points believers to Christ
He glorifies Himself so believers will worship the Spirit
He draws attention to the gifts He distributes
He elevates the five-fold ministry offices
correct_answer: He glorifies Jesus and points believers to Christ

question_text: The module lists steps for how to receive the Holy Spirit. According to the text, what is the very first step?
answer_options: Be born again, since the Holy Spirit is given to those who belong to Christ through faith
Speak in other tongues as the Spirit gives utterance
Work hard to earn the Spirit through obedience
Yield yourself and allow Him to lead your life
correct_answer: Be born again, since the Holy Spirit is given to those who belong to Christ through faith

question_text: The text groups the nine gifts of the Spirit into three categories: Revelation, Power, and Inspiration. Which gift belongs to the Gifts of Power?
answer_options: The Working of Miracles
The Word of Knowledge
Prophecy
Interpretation of Tongues

question_text: The module contrasts the nine gifts of the Spirit with the five-fold ministry offices. Which description matches the nine gifts rather than the offices?
answer_options: Given to all believers, for momentary manifestation, operating as the Spirit wills
Given to some believers, for ongoing ministry function, operating as a calling
Given to some believers and earned through striving over time
Given to all believers as a permanent office held for life
correct_answer: Given to all believers, for momentary manifestation, operating as the Spirit wills

question_text: The text explains why Paul uses the singular "fruit" and not "fruits." What does it say this teaches about the Spirit's work?
answer_options: It is one unified work with nine manifestations that grow together, so maturity means balanced development across all nine
It means a believer may pick which fruits to develop one at a time
It means the fruit is about external behavior modification rather than nature
It means each fruit is a separate gift distributed as the Spirit wills
correct_answer: It is one unified work with nine manifestations that grow together, so maturity means balanced development across all nine
